steps: Gently detach cells from the tissue culture flask in the following manner:
Wash with 2 mL PBS, then aspirate.
Resuspend the cells in 10 mL of DMEM with FBS. Aliquot into a centrifuge tube. Of course, if there is a T25 flask, resuspend in lesser volume.

Aliquot a small amount of the mixture (about 200 µL ) for cell counting. Ideally, cell viability should be in excess of 90% in order to achieve a good recovery after freezing. Keep at -4 °C - We need 1 x 10 6 - 5 x 10 6 cells for freezing.
Centrifuge the cell suspensions at 1500rpm for 00:05:00
Add about 3 mL of freezing media into the tube and re-suspend.
Freezing media preparation: - 10% DMSO, 20% FBS, 70% appropriate cell culture medium (with serum + antibiotics) (e.g 100uL of DMSO + 200uL FBS + 700uL cell culture medium with FBS and antibiotics)
Dispense the aliquots of cell suspension into the cryogenic storage vials.
Cool the vials gradually as follows: -20 °C for 01:00:00 -80 °C overnight Liquid Nitrogen
creator: PMAT0001
